response:
<<THE MAXIMUM LIKELIHOOD ESTIMATORS FOR THE PARAMETERS>>

THE MAXIMUM LIKELIHOOD ESTIMATORS FOR THE PARAMETERS ARE AS FOLLOWS:

THE MLE FOR MU_X IS THE SAMPLE MEAN OF THE PRE-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES [X1, X2, ..., X50]

THE MLE FOR MU_Y IS THE SAMPLE MEAN OF THE POST-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES [Y1, Y2, ..., Y50]

THE MLE FOR SIGMA_X2 IS THE SAMPLE VARIANCE OF THE PRE-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES [X1, X2, ..., X50]

THE MLE FOR SIGMA_Y2 IS THE SAMPLE VARIANCE OF THE POST-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES [Y1, Y2, ..., Y50]

THE MLE FOR SIGMA_XY IS THE SAMPLE COVARIANCE BETWEEN THE PRE-TREATMENT AND POST-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES [X1, X2, ..., X50] AND [Y1, Y2, ..., Y50]

TO TEST THE HYPOTHESIS THAT THE DRUG HAS A SIGNIFICANT EFFECT ON REDUCING BLOOD PRESSURE, WE CAN USE A T-TEST OR A CHI-SQUARE TEST.

THE NULL HYPOTHESIS IS THAT THE DRUG HAS NO EFFECT ON BLOOD PRESSURE [H0: MU_X - MU_Y = 0]

THE ALTERNATIVE HYPOTHESIS IS THAT THE DRUG DOES HAVE AN EFFECT ON BLOOD PRESSURE [H1: MU_X - MU_Y ≠ 0]

THE TEST STATISTIC IS THE T-STATISTIC [T = (MU_X - MU_Y) / SE]

THE SE IS THE STANDARD ERROR OF THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN THE MEANS [SE = sqrt(SIGMA_X2 / n + SIGMA_Y2 / n)]

THE REJECTION CRITERIA IS THAT IF THE ABSOLUTE VALUE OF THE T-STATISTIC IS GREATER THAN THE CRITICAL VALUE [T critical] THEN WE REJECT THE NULL HYPOTHESIS.

THE CRITICAL VALUE FOR A TWO-TAILED TEST AT ALPHA = 0.05 IS [T critical = 2.0096]

THE REJECTION CRITERIA IS THAT IF THE ABSOLUTE VALUE OF THE T-STATISTIC IS GREATER THAN [T critical] THEN WE REJECT THE NULL HYPOTHESIS.
